directive:
OPEN-OBLIGATION DISCHARGE for thm:no-honest-adaptation (the conditional Le Cam reduction lem:conditional-two-alpha-length / lem:alpha-rate-separation / lem:conditional-two-alpha-adaptation-reduction is ALREADY PROVED; the ONLY missing piece is the two-alpha least-favorable PAIR construction). Construct the pair from PRIMITIVE CCOT PRTE laws via the boundary analogue of the Cai-Low (2004/2005) two-point / between-modulus adaptation lower bound (also Low 1997; boundary/frontier two-point: Hall-Park, Daouia-Florens-Simar 2010). DO NOT assume the pair; CONSTRUCT it. Recipe:
Instantiate a triangular primitive Roy/CCOT subclass with X degenerate, B=p(Z)-p_L in (0,bar_b), p(Z)=p_L+B, D|B~Bernoulli(p_L+B), constant active face, strict overlap away from p_L, and fixed outcome/transport primitives whose boundary loading is chi=R in {-1,1}. Under P0n let f0(b)=c alpha0 b^{alpha0-1} near 0. Choose mark law with E(R|B=b)=0 on b<=n^{-1}, E(R|B=b)=mu>0 on [n^{-1},h_n], and E(R^2|B=b)=1; this gives clean alpha0 tail, stabilized marks, and nonzero shell variance. Let l_n=n^{-1/alpha0} (or smaller if alpha0=1), r_n=n^{-1}, h_n=n^{-beta}, 0<beta<1. Define P1n by changing only the conditional density of B: on (0,l_n) replace f0 by c_n alpha1 b^{alpha1-1} with c_n chosen to preserve the P0n mass of (0,l_n), so P1n has exact tail exponent alpha1 as t downarrow 0; outside (0,h_n) keep f0. On A_n=[r_n,h_n] add the least-favorable tilt f1=f0{1+eps_n s_n}, renormalized inside A_n, where g_n(b)=mu b^{-1/2}, V_n=E0[g_n(B)^2 1_A_n], s_n=(g_n-E0[g_n|A_n])/sqrt(V_n), eps_n=a/sqrt(n). Positivity holds for small a. Core Hellinger is C l_n^{alpha0}; tilt Hellinger is eps_n^2 V_n/(8 V_n)+o(n^{-1})=a^2/(8n)+o(n^{-1}), hence nH^2<=C_core+a^2/8 and choose a,C_core so TV(P0n^n,P1n^n)<=eta. The endpoint expansion gives Gamma(P1n)-Gamma(P0n)=eps_n sqrt(V_n)+o(rho_n(alpha0)^{-1}). Since V_n~C n^{1-alpha0} for alpha0<1 and V_n~C(1-beta)log n for alpha0=1, separation is Delta/rho_n(alpha0).

Emit these lemmas (prove each, then lem:two-alpha-pair-exists discharges the obligation and closes the theorem via the proved reduction):
  - lem:primitive-roy-boundary-subclass-nondegenerate: For every 0<alpha0<=1<alpha1 there exist triangular generalized-Roy/CCOT PRTE laws with B-density f0(b)=c alpha0 b^{alpha0-1}, constant active face, dual-gap separation, stabilized marks, and nonzero boundary loading variance, so P0n in P_n(alpha0) and the core-modified law P1n has exact lower-boundary exponent alpha1.  [depends_on: def:ccot-lower-endpoint, def:boundary-class, ass:boundary-tail, ass:boundary-mark-stabilization, ass:centered-shell-variance, ass:unique-active-face, ass:dual-gap-scale]  Proof: Take X degenerate, p(Z)=p_L+B, Bernoulli treatment, and fixed outcome/OT primitives yielding unique active face and chi=R. Direct density integration gives the alpha tails; E(R^2|B)=1 gives K_alpha shell variance; constant face/gap are primitive by construction.
  - lem:boundary-density-tilt-hellinger: For the above P0n and P1n with core width l_n and annular tilt eps_n=a/sqrt(n), H^2(P0n,P1n)=O(l_n^{alpha0})+a^2/(8n)+o(n^{-1}); hence a and l_n may be chosen so TV(P0n^n,P1n^n)<=eta for any eta<1.  [depends_on: lem:primitive-roy-boundary-subclass-nondegenerate]  Proof: Use the identity H^2=int(sqrt f1-sqrt f0)^2/2. The power-core replacement contributes a constant multiple of its original mass l_n^{alpha0}. Taylor expansion of sqrt(1+eps_n s_n) gives eps_n^2 E0[s_n^2]/8+o(eps_n^2). Tensorize Hellinger and bound TV by product Hellinger.
  - lem:two-alpha-pair-exists: Under the primitive boundary endpoint expansion, the constructed laws satisfy |theta_L(P0n)-theta_L(P1n)|>=Delta/rho_n(alpha0) and TV(P0n^n,P1n^n)<=eta for fixed Delta>0 and eta<1-2delta.  [depends_on: lem:primitive-roy-boundary-subclass-nondegenerate, lem:boundary-density-tilt-hellinger, def:alpha-normalization, thm:endpoint-limit-law, lem:conditional-two-alpha-adaptation-reduction]  Proof: Apply the active-face first-order expansion Gamma(P1n)-Gamma(P0n)=int chi B^{-1/2} d(P1n-P0n)+o(rho_n(alpha0)^{-1}). The core has zero mean loading, while the annular tilt contributes eps_n sqrt(V_n). Evaluate V_n=int_{r_n}^{h_n} mu^2 b^{-1} f0(b)db: it is asymptotic to C n^{1-alpha0} for alpha0<1 and C(1-beta)log n for alpha0=1. Thus eps_n sqrt(V_n) is exactly the slow rate.
Key facts to verify in the proofs: (i) BOTH members are genuinely valid CCOT PRTE laws in their tail classes P_n(alpha_0), P_n(alpha_1) (non-degeneracy exhibited, not assumed); (ii) n*H^2(P0n,P1n)=O(1) via core l_n^{alpha0}+tilt a^2/(8n) => TV(P0n^n,P1n^n)<=eta<1-2delta; (iii) endpoint displacement Gamma(P1n)-Gamma(P0n)=eps_n*sqrt(V_n) with V_n~n^{1-alpha0} (alpha0<1) or (1-beta)log n (alpha0=1), giving |theta_L(P0n)-theta_L(P1n)|>=Delta/rho_n(alpha0). Set Delta=tau/2 for n large.

If liminf_{n -> infinity} inf_{P in P_n(alpha_0) union P_n(alpha_1)} P_P( theta_L in C_n ) >= 1 - delta, then there exists c > 0 such that liminf_{n -> infinity} sup_{P in P_n(alpha_1)} rho_n(alpha_0) E_P[ |C_n| ] >= c.
